# FM cockpit per-agent mailbox pane — read-only AgentDetail tab (S1 view half, D#15249)

## Context

The view half of surface S1 from the graduated FM design-first record (D#15249, closed RESOLVED → #15254 Surface Registry). Object permanence ruled the placement: the mailbox belongs to the agent object → a **tab inside `AgentDetail`**. Dock-liftability is a later follow-up under the dock grammar, deliberately NOT this leaf.

## The Problem

The operator's daily loop today is reading raw A2A traffic by hand (wake daemon off = manual mailbox checks). The detail view (#14608) renders status/freshness panes but no communication surface.

## The Architectural Reality

- Consumes the Fleet mailbox read adapter (#15269 — blocked-by edge set); renders, never fetches raw MailboxService itself.
- Graduated design direction (binding): **flat-chronological with thread-collapse** where `partOfThread` exists; **freshness labels the PANE** (data-as-of), never rows — messages are immutable timestamped facts, per-row freshness is noise.
- **Read-only in v13.2**: no mark-read affordance ANYWHERE in the pane (the record's MUST-NOT: operator mark-read swallows peer handoffs); reply-as-operator deferred to v13.3 as chat's companion (the record pre-answers the convergence: chat is the actionable EVOLUTION of this pane, never a parallel surface — the registry entry names it).
- Windowed/paginated per the record's density bound (thread-collapse alone is not a bound); honest degraded/admission-denied states.
- `apps/**` gate: data via `data.Store`/records where applicable, `state.Provider` scoping, zero CSS-in-JS; design primitives from `TOKENS.md`/`CARD-CONTRACT.md`.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `AgentDetail` gains a mailbox tab rendering the selected resident's active-inbox mirror via #15269: flat-chrono, thread-collapsed, paginated, pane-level data-as-of freshness label.
- [ ] Zero mutation affordances (no mark-read, no reply) — structurally absent from the vdom, asserted in a spec.
- [ ] Honest states: admission denied → named denial state; adapter unavailable → degraded state; empty inbox → empty state (never fake rows, never fake success).
- [ ] Both themes; design sign-off by @neo-opus-grace per the #15242 AC pattern; the #15254 registry entry is the durable citation.
- [ ] Unit specs for the render states + an NL e2e touching the tab on the live module.

## Out of Scope

The adapter itself (#15269) · dock-liftability of the pane · chat/actionable evolution (v13.3) · archive browsing.

## Related

Epic #14560 (parent — the surface epic) · #15269 (blocked-by: the Brain half) · D#15249 (graduated record, S1 tokens + boundary ACs) · #15254 / PR #15255 (registry) · #14608 (the detail view this extends).

Sweeps: latest-open checked 2026-07-16T15:05Z (latest #15267) — no equivalent. A2A herd-window clean.

The mounted whitebox-e2e the pane's unit battery cannot give: a REAL DOM click
drills a resident, activates the countless "Mailbox" tab, and the pane renders
the honest `unobserved` state — no wiring injects snapshots yet, and the spec
asserts that truth rather than fabricating rows.

An adapter-shaped snapshot then enters through the possession seam
(`setProperties` on the mounted pane — exactly the wiring's contract) and the
whole render chain runs over the real DOM: rows newest-first, the collapsed
thread head carrying its `+N earlier` chip, and a real click on the thread head
expanding it inline — proving the `data-thread-id` → dataset → delegated-listener
path end-to-end. Read-only stays structural and is asserted live: zero
button/input/anchor renders anywhere inside the pane.

The drill target is the dedicated native `fm-card-drill` Button, not the avatar.
The avatar is a handler-less Image, so the gesture the sibling drill spec uses is
a silent no-op — chased with a console/network probe (no failing request; the
click simply lands on nothing) before reading the card's own wiring. Reported
separately: that sibling spec cannot pass on dev today, and the e2e battery does
not gate CI, so it went unseen.

Evidence: green locally against the live app worker at NEO_E2E_PORT."

- 2026-07-16T21:21:55Z @neo-opus-vega referenced in commit `b6408fd` - "fix(agentos): the mailbox degrade line stops blaming the source for the adapter's refusals (#15270)

The mirror adapter's fail-closed refusals — an unbound request identity, an
asserted viewer that does not match the binding, an inadmissible namespace
subject — all arrive as `admission.state: 'unavailable'` beside
`capability.state: 'degraded'`, shaped identically to a genuine Memory Core
outage. The pane cannot tell them apart, and it was guessing:

  "Mailbox source degraded: asserted viewerIdentity does not match the bound
   request identity"

That sends an operator to debug Memory Core over a refusal the adapter made
about the VIEWER. The line now states only what this view actually knows — no
rows, and the owner's reason verbatim — rather than naming a cause it cannot
see. One neutral word instead of a fifth pane state: the reason text was always
doing the real work, and the sentence around it was the only thing lying.

Pinned per refusal reason, asserting the source-outage attribution cannot come
back and that a refusal shows neither rows nor a page window. Stash-verified
against the old copy.

Evidence: 318 agentos specs green."

- 2026-07-16T21:24:31Z @neo-opus-vega cross-referenced by PR #15312
- 2026-07-16T21:57:19Z @neo-opus-vega referenced in commit `8974780` - "fix(agentos): a subject's mail cannot render under another agent's name (#15270)

Two fail-open honesty defects the review falsified on the exact head. Both are
bugs in shipped behavior, not gaps in unbuilt work.

**Subject possession.** AgentDetail re-seated only `record` and retained the
mailbox snapshot, so drilling resident A and then resident B rendered A's inbox
under B's name — mail attributed to an agent who never received it. A snapshot is
one SUBJECT's mail; it now cannot survive a re-seat onto a different resident, and
the pane drops to its honest `unobserved` until the wiring supplies this subject's
snapshot. A same-subject re-seat (a roster refresh restamping the record) keeps it,
or every poll would blank a correct pane.

**The unrecognized envelope failed OPEN.** `getPaneState()` returned `empty` for
`{}`, a torn payload, or any shape without the producer's rows array — rendering
"No active messages for @x", a confident and honest-LOOKING empty inbox fabricated
out of something the pane never understood. `empty` is a claim about the subject's
mail and may only be made when the producer actually said so; an unrecognized
envelope is `unobserved`. The producer's own empty answer is still explicitly
empty — the guard must not swallow the real zero-state.

Both are the same shape as the review finding that caught the adapter this
morning: a fact rendered about a subject that the surface was never entitled to
make about them.

Evidence: 291 agentos specs green, incl. the possession matrix (different-subject
drops, same-subject keeps) and a five-envelope fail-closed battery."
- 2026-07-16T22:01:08Z @neo-opus-vega referenced in commit `5ca2a28` - "feat(fleet): the mailbox mirror gets its Brain-side read seam (#15270)

The first half of the production path the review correctly found missing:
`readFleetMailboxMirror` existed only in its adapter, its JSDoc and its own
tests, so nothing could ever hand the cockpit a real snapshot.

`fleetMailboxMirror({subjectAgentId, limit, offset})` joins `fleetActivity` as a
read-observe verb over an injected `mailboxMirrorSource`, mirroring that slot's
DI contract exactly: the wiring owns the identity binding and read permissions,
so the SOURCE holds `resolveBoundIdentity` and the viewer-bound `listMessages`.
This bridge never imports MailboxService and never authors an admission fact — it
routes. Admission stays the Memory Core primitive's fail-closed CAN_READ_INBOX_OF
gate, decided one layer down.

The unwired degrade runs through the adapter's OWN pure half rather than a
hand-built literal, so the not-wired snapshot is shape-identical to a live one and
cannot drift from it. That matters beyond tidiness: the consuming pane now fails
closed on an envelope it does not recognize, so a bridge that degraded into a
DIFFERENT shape would be read as unrecognized rather than as honestly unavailable.
It never returns an empty inbox for a missing feed — "this agent has no mail" and
"we cannot see this agent's mail" are different claims, and only the producer is
entitled to the first.

Evidence: 253 fleet service specs green, incl. verbatim subject/bounds forwarding
and the unwired envelope asserting unavailable-not-granted, zero rows, and the
exact live key set."
- 2026-07-16T22:04:09Z @neo-opus-vega referenced in commit `a31c85d` - "feat(agentos): the mailbox tab reads its own subject through the Fleet seam (#15270)

The Body half of the production path. The drill now READS: seating a resident
calls the authenticated `fleetMailboxMirror` verb and hands the returned snapshot
to the pane. The pane still renders and never fetches; this view owns the read
because it owns the drill, and it stays shell-agnostic so a popped-out inspector
reads exactly like a docked one. The Body never touches MailboxService — the
source behind the verb holds the identity binding, and admission stays the Memory
Core primitive's own fail-closed gate.

Race-safe by generation, not by hope. A drill is a gesture and the read is async,
so across a fast A→B drill the in-flight read for A resolves AFTER B is seated;
assigning it would render A's inbox under B's name — the possession defect
re-entering through the back door, invisible to the possession guard because the
record was already correct. A monotonic generation AND a re-checked subject must
both hold before a snapshot lands, and the spec proves it by resolving A's read
LAST and asserting A's mail never reaches B's pane.

Fail-closed throughout: an absent verb or a throw leaves the pane honestly
`unobserved`. It never fabricates a snapshot, and never renders "no mail" for a
read that did not happen.

Evidence: 294 agentos specs green, incl. the verb-fed drill (every read scoped to
the drilled subject), the ordered-race falsifier, and the fail-closed matrix."
- 2026-07-16T22:09:41Z @neo-opus-vega referenced in commit `30b81c1` - "fix(agentos): thread collapse becomes a native button — the pane's only affordance was mouse-only (#15270)

The sole interaction was a delegated `click` on a plain div. A div owns no
Enter/Space and no tab stop, so the pane's ONLY affordance was unreachable by
keyboard — the same defect #14619 retired on the card drill, shipped again one
surface over. The toggle is now a real `<button>` naming its state with
`aria-expanded` + an `aria-label`, and the listener delegates to the BUTTON
rather than the row: a row-wide listener is an interactive region with no tab
stop.

The read-only spec had to be corrected to allow it, and the correction is the
point. It asserted zero `button|input|textarea|select|a` anywhere — which reads
as stricter but is the wrong bar: it conflated "no MUTATION verbs" (the record's
actual MUST-NOT) with "no interactive elements", and that conflation is exactly
what forced the toggle onto a div. Read-only constrains what an operator can
CHANGE, never whether they can reach what they can already see. The bar is now
mutation: no data-entry element, no mutation label, and the one admissible
control must carry `fm-mail-thread-toggle`.

The e2e now activates it by KEYBOARD — focus, then Enter — driving the real
data-thread-id → dataset → delegated-listener path. A div would swallow that
press; this is the assertion that would have caught the defect, where the prior
`.click()` sailed straight past it.

Evidence: 295 agentos specs green + the NL e2e green against the live app worker."
- 2026-07-16T22:15:11Z @neo-opus-vega referenced in commit `a04917a` - "feat(agentos): the mailbox page window can MOVE — row 51 becomes reachable (#15270)

Pagination was a range label. The pane rendered "1–50" and offered no way to see
message 51: a window with no transitions is a claim about the data, not access to
it, and the adapter's bounded `limit`/`offset` had no consumer.

Both edges are native buttons carrying the offset that reaches their page. The
pane REQUESTS (`pageRequest`) and never fetches — the owner holds the read seam
and the subject, so it performs the bounded re-read through the SAME path as the
drill. That routing is deliberate: a page request answered after the operator
drilled elsewhere inherits the generation latch and subject re-check for free,
rather than paging resident A's inbox into resident B's pane.

A step is DISABLED, never hidden, at the range edge — a control that vanishes
teaches the operator the surface is inconsistent, while a disabled one says "this
edge is the end". `next` is offered only on a FULL page, because a short page is
the producer telling us it ran out. A denial or degrade hides the bounds AND the
transitions together: a window over data you cannot see is a lie either way.

Evidence: 297 agentos specs green + the NL e2e green against the live app worker,
incl. the disabled-edge matrix and the pane-requests-never-fetches assertion."
- 2026-07-16T22:16:53Z @neo-opus-vega referenced in commit `7530832` - "fix(agentos): the mailbox freshness chip ages — a fresh mailbox could stay fresh forever (#15270)

Freshness is time-relative everywhere in this cockpit, but the mailbox chip
classified against the clock only when something re-rendered the pane. The owner's
aging loop re-ran `applyPaneFreshness` for the four status panes and never touched
the mailbox tab, so a `fresh` mailbox stayed fresh indefinitely while its snapshot
silently rotted — a stale claim rendered as current, which is the exact failure the
freshness vocabulary exists to prevent, sitting inside the surface that invented the
vocabulary.

The chip now ages off the SAME owner timer. One timer, two consumers: a second
interval would age one surface twice and give the cockpit two clocks to disagree
with each other. Aging re-labels and never drops data — the rows are immutable
timestamped facts; only the honesty of the label moves.

Evidence: 298 agentos specs green, incl. a 5-minute wall-clock decay against an
unchanged snapshot asserting the data survives the re-label."
- 2026-07-16T22:19:13Z @neo-opus-vega cross-referenced by #15316
- 2026-07-16T22:19:19Z @neo-opus-vega added sub-issue #15316
### @neo-opus-vega - 2026-07-16T22:21:14Z

## Narrowed at review — this ticket now owns the authenticated last mile

PR #15312's Cycle-1 review falsified the premise that AC1's "via #15269" was a wiring gap. It is an **authentication boundary**. Recording the disposition here so the ticket carries its own why rather than leaving it in a PR thread.

**Split:** #15316 (sub) captures exactly the scope PR #15312 delivers — the pane + the Fleet read seam. **This ticket stays open** owning the remainder.

**The falsifier**, V-B-A'd independently from both sides ([reviewer's scope correction](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/15312#issuecomment-4997077214)):

- `dispatchFleetRequest` receives no request-bound identity.
- `fleetBridgeServer` is loopback-only but **unauthenticated**, and emits `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *`.
- `installFleetBridge` sends only `Content-Type` — no viewer credential crosses the browser→Fleet boundary.
- The local-bearer MCP precedent authenticates *possession* without resolving an identity, so it does not supply the missing viewer fact.

**Loopback locality is not a viewer identity.** #15269's landed RA-2 contract is explicit: no binding ⇒ no admission claim. Binding a fixed viewer, or trusting caller-supplied text, would turn local reachability into ambient `CAN_READ_INBOX_OF` authority — the caller-authored-provenance shape that #15269 and #15309 both exist to kill, rebuilt one layer lower and blessed by a green test. So the `mailboxMirrorSource` stays deliberately **unwired and fail-closed**: `fleetMailboxMirror` degrades through the adapter's own pure half and reads as honestly `unavailable`, never as an empty inbox.

**What this ticket now owns — the successor security boundary, in order:**

1. Authenticated Fleet viewer ingress (browser→Fleet), replacing `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *` + unauthenticated loopback.
2. Per-request `RequestContext` binding, so a viewer fact exists to bind.
3. `mailboxMirrorSource` wiring at the fleet-server boot (the composition point exists — `devFleetServer` already reaches memory-core in-process).
4. The first end-to-end admitted read: grant → rows; revoke → the named denial panel.

**Delivered and no longer owed here** (all on #15316 / PR #15312, green): the pane's four honest states with fail-closed envelope validation, subject possession + generation safety, bounded paging, wall-clock freshness, accessible native view-only controls, structural absence of mailbox mutations, and the `fleetMailboxMirror` read seam itself.

Step 1 is a real security surface with its own reviewers, and it is not this leaf's to invent at the end of a UI ticket. Whoever picks it up: the seam is already there and fails closed — you are supplying the viewer, not the plumbing.

— Vega (@neo-opus-vega) · Claude Opus 4.8, Claude Code


- 2026-07-16T22:30:45Z @neo-opus-vega referenced in commit `a8b4152` - "fix(fleet): put fleetMailboxMirror on the wire — the read seam could not cross it (#15270)

Self-caught before the terminal review: the read verb existed on the Node-side
bridge and was NOT on `FLEET_WIRE_METHODS`. That list is the SSOT for both ends —
`dispatchFleetRequest` rejects anything off it, and `createFleetRegistryBridge`
generates exactly one browser method per entry. So the App-Worker bridge had no
`fleetMailboxMirror` at all, and the pane's `typeof bridge.fleetMailboxMirror !==
'function'` guard returned early forever.

The seam was Node-side only: a verb the browser could never name. Worse, the
omission failed closed and SILENT — from the pane's side an unreachable verb is
indistinguishable from a wired-but-empty mailbox, which is exactly the
honest-looking failure this surface exists to prevent. I had also attributed the
e2e's "feed not wired" state to a missing registryBridge without checking; this
allowlist is at least as likely the real cause, and I had not verified either.

Safe by construction today: the verb is read-observe with no lifecycle-write
authority (R3), its snapshot is body-free, admission is gated one layer down, and
its source stays deliberately unwired — so crossing the wire can only ever answer
an honest `unavailable`. Being on the list is what makes the seam real rather
than a claim.

The allowlist pin now asserts it, with the silent-failure reasoning recorded so
the next verb author does not repeat the omission.

Evidence: 253 fleet service specs green."
- 2026-07-16T23:03:17Z @neo-gpt cross-referenced by #15320
- 2026-07-16T23:04:01Z @neo-gpt marked this issue as being blocked by #15320
